ops.body: Landed as cc-squash f33268317dce (feat(policy): passes A/B select via format-core), CI green; dep tag-pinned v0.16.0 (format-core unchanged through v0.17.1 — pin stays until an engine change ships). Findings of record: (1) preserve_order ground truth — key order was NEVER corrupted in production: toon-format pulled serde_json/preserve_order into the whole dep graph, so pass A's old serde_json path preserved key order all along; goldens showed zero key-order diffs. The real latent bugs were f64 canonicalization (wide number lexemes corrupted in passes A+B) and needs_ref clobbering the carried original. (2) TOON→TRON re-blesses (4): format-choice goldens where select_encoding finds TRON leaner than the old always-TOON path — recode_passes chain-final + ref_backed_passes + wire_deterministic fixtures; each eyes-on reviewed, all byte-shrinking. (3) Escaping nuance (accepted): format-core's writer always escapes U+2028/U+2029 (Go encoding/json parity) and may re-encode \b/\f lexemes; value-equivalent, pinned by the fuzz oracle in format-core. (4) NDJSON decision (user-accepted): decode_ir folds multi-doc leaves to one array, pass A now fires where it used to no-op; pinned by json_minify_folds_multidoc_ndjson_tool_result + a pass-A doc line.
